mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and corporate revenue breadth, causing higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; negative payroll momentum reverses the signal.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower PAYEMS growth in more than 55% of walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, restrictive discount rates reduce the present value of equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS-above-4.5% observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL momentum combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% signals inflation pressure that prevents near-term easing, raising the equity risk premium and causing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth is positive and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-positive-momentum and FEDFUNDS-above-4.5% state has no lower mean 20-day return than all other states at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month acceleration in RSAFS signals resilient household demand and stronger near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the sales signal is observed.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth crossings above 0% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising 6-month INDPRO growth signals improving operating leverage and industrial earnings breadth, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when production momentum remains above zero.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 6-month growth does not deliver higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than nonpositive INDPRO growth in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage point over three months signals deteriorating labor demand and recession-risk repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rises above the trigger and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: Falling HOUST combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% signals that restrictive rates are suppressing interest-sensitive demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-decline and FEDFUNDS-above-4.5% state is not associated with lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods without that joint state.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 2-quarter growth is positive while UNRATE remains below 4%, the economy is in a low-unemployment expansion that supports earnings expectations, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered GDPC1-positive-growth and UNRATE-below-4% observations have a lower mean 20-day S&P 500 return than untriggered observations in out-of-sample tests.
